location.office_time_required: Mondays, Tuesdays and Thursdays (Anchor Days); certain teams may require additional in-office workdays

level_signals: 5+ years working in Customer Operations and/or Strategy
Senior presence -- can convey complex information to executives clearly and concisely
Ownership -- high ownership mentality demonstrated by developing projects of >6-12 months length
Base salary range $205,000 - $230,000 consistent with Senior IC in San Francisco

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years working in Customer Operations and/or Strategy in a high growth SaaS model
Strong analytical skills -- can model any operational question full cycle (develop assumptions and output)
AI-native approach to building workflows, insights, and end-to-end systems between humans & agents
High EQ -- works well with stakeholders, gains trust, enjoys working with people
Senior presence -- can convey complex information to executives clearly and concisely; can resolve tradeoffs. Effective communicator across levels and audiences
First principles problem solver -- dimensionalizes problems, demonstrates history of lateral thinking
Hands on -- extensive experience and interest in building from scratch
Ownership -- high ownership mentality demonstrated by developing projects of >6-12 months length
Notion power user or desire to learn -- prior Notion experience is not required, but you have the ability and desire to become a pro

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Customer-facing experience on an account team (Onboarding, CS, Account Management, etc.)
Experience with statistical methods, SQL, Tableau, Python and/or other advanced analytics and BI/Dashboarding
Experience using Zendesk, Gainsight and Salesforce
History of promotions or progressive responsibilities at work

scope.surface: Customer Success team operations, covering onboarding, adoption, retention, and growth phases of the customer journey globally
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS (PLG + enterprise), collaborative AI workspace
primary_metrics_inferred: CS team productivity and capacity utilization
Customer health scores and leading risk indicators
core_responsibilities: Design and optimize the end-to-end customer journey including milestones, success metrics, and playbooks
Develop analyses that help design, set and measure strategy for global Customer Success and Customer Experience
Design, build and manage resource allocation model globally by segment
Develop and mature a rigorous, consistent, predictable operating rhythm and KPI reviews
Design and maintain core processes (attainment reporting, forecast vs. actuals, At Risk Account Reviews)
Partner with Data and BizTech to streamline daily team operations and build AI workflows

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned generically alongside cash compensation and benefits

green_flags: Compensation range explicitly disclosed ($205K-$230K base for SF)
Equity and benefits mentioned alongside cash compensation
Clear scope of ownership and responsibilities described in detail
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every bullet
Role sits at the strategic core of a high-growth, well-known SaaS company
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires in-office presence Monday, Tuesday, Thursday every week — significant time commitment and SF-only
Role title says 'Manager' but no people management signals are present — seniority level may be ambiguous
Visa sponsorship is not addressed in the JD
BA/BS listed as 'required' with no equivalency clause, which may unnecessarily filter candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, scope, and 'senior presence' language)
seniority.manages_people (not stated; inferred as false based on absence of any people management language)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and customer references)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed in JD)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from anchor day policy and #LI-Onsite tag)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred; JD states range is 'for roles based in San Francisco')
ambiguities: Role title uses 'Manager' but the JD body refers to the role as 'CS Operations Lead' — these may not be equivalent and exact seniority/people-management expectations are unclear.
Visa sponsorship is not mentioned anywhere in the JD; treated as 'unclear'.
The 'BA / BS required' line has no 'or equivalent experience' caveat, unlike other requirements — treated as hard, but the general encouragement to apply may imply some flexibility.
